Experiment 5: Main Models RSA — Cosine Distance

6-predictor regression: neural RDM ~ βA + βB + βC + βD + βE + βF + error

Model: LLaMA-2-13B-Chat | Metric: Cosine | Permutations: 10,000 | FDR: Benjamini-Hochberg (q=0.05)

Table of Contents

  • 1. Stimulus Design
  • 2. Sample Stimulus
  • 3. Model Specification
  • 4. VIF Check & Model Correlations
  • 5. Model A: Beta & Unique Variance
  • 6. All Model Betas
  • 7. Full Results Table
  • 8. Model RDMs
  • 9. Neural RDMs at Peak Layers

1. Stimulus Design

56 items × 6 conditions = 336 sentences. Subject fixed to "He" throughout. Each item has a mental state verb, a matched action verb, and a shared object noun across all 6 conditions.

CodeLabelTemplateExample
C1mental_stateHe [mental verb] the [object].He notices the crack.
C2dis_mental[Mental verb] the [object].Notice the crack.
C3scr_mentalThe [object] to [mental verb].The crack to notice.
C4actionHe [action verb] the [object].He fills the crack.
C5dis_action[Action verb] the [object].Fill the crack.
C6scr_actionThe [object] to [action verb].The crack to fill.

Condition Feature Matrix

FeatureC1C2C3C4C5C6
Has subject "He"✓✓
Mental verb present✓✓✓
Action verb present✓✓✓
Grammatical word order✓✓✓✓
Scrambled form✓✓
Object noun present✓✓✓✓✓✓
Mental verb + object (grammatical)✓✓
Action verb + object (grammatical)✓✓

7 verb categories × 8 items each: Attention, Memory, Sensation, Belief, Desire, Emotion, Intention.

2. Sample Stimulus

Item 1 (Attention): mental verb = notices, action verb = fills, object = the crack

ConditionSentence
C1 (mental_state)He notices the crack.
C2 (dis_mental)Notice the crack.
C3 (scr_mental)The crack to notice.
C4 (action)He fills the crack.
C5 (dis_action)Fill the crack.
C6 (scr_action)The crack to fill.

3. Model Specification

ModelNamePredicts similarity when both in...Purpose
AFull Attribution{C1}Target: bound subject + mental verb + object
BMental Verb Presence{C1, C2, C3}Controls for mental vocabulary clustering
CSubject Presence{C1, C4}Controls for having an agent/subject
DItem IdentitySame item (all 6 conditions)Controls for shared object noun / word overlap
EMental Verb + Object{C1, C2}Controls for grammatical mental-verb-object binding without subject
FGrammatical Order{C1, C2, C4, C5}Controls for well-formed vs scrambled
neural_RDM = βA + βB + βC + βD + βE + βF + error

βA significant means: C1 pairs are more similar in the model's representations than what you'd predict from any additive combination of sharing a mental verb (B), sharing a subject (C), sharing an item (D), sharing a grammatical mental-verb+object frame (E), or sharing grammatical order (F).

Interpretation caveat: βA cannot distinguish "bound propositional attribution" from "surface feature conjunction detection" because C1 is the only condition where subject + mental verb + object co-occur in grammatical order. The interchange intervention analysis (separate pipeline) addresses this gap.

4. VIF Check & Model Correlations

Variance Inflation Factors

VIF < 5: OK. VIF 5–10: proceed with caution. VIF > 10: regression not trustworthy.

ModelVIFStatus
Model A: Full Attribution1.72OK
Model B: Mental Verb Presence1.80OK
Model C: Subject Presence1.58OK
Model D: Item Identity1.00OK
Model E: Mental Verb + Object2.52OK
Model F: Grammatical Order1.58OK

ABCDEF

B0.2911.000-0.001-0.0140.612-0.001

E0.4760.6120.154-0.0171.0000.394

5. Model A: Beta & Unique Variance

Significance markers: * pFDR < .05   ** pFDR < .01   *** pFDR < .001   △ puncorr < .05 (not FDR-corrected)

6. All Model Betas

All 6 model betas from the simultaneous regression at each layer. Filled dots indicate FDR-corrected significance (pFDR < .05).

8. Model RDMs

6 binary model RDMs (336×336). Each predicts similarity (0) for sentence pairs where both belong to the specified condition set.

9. Neural RDMs at Peak Layers

Neural RDMs (correlation distance) at the most significant layer for each model predictor. Rows and columns are organized by condition.

Generated by code/rsa/6_predictors/2_main_models_report.py
